identificationModule.briefTitle: Beta Agonist Nebulization in Non Invasively Ventilated COPD Patients: Safety, and Therapeutic Efficacy Range.
identificationModule.officialTitle: Controlled Randomized Double-blind Study Comparing Salbutamol and Placebo Via Aerosol in Chronic Obstructive Respiratory Insufficiency in Exacerbartion Treated With Noninvasive Ventilation

descriptionModule.briefSummary:
randomized double-blind controlled study in parallel groups

Salbutamol is a β2 mimetic short-acting to be administered by nebulization in this study.

During this administration, non invasive ventilation for the patient will be continued.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
Randomization will focus on the treatment administered (placebo or salbutamol ).

After the first phase , a switch will be set up for each patient , retaining the blind it will be administered salbutamol (for those who received placebo in the first phase ) or placebo (for those receiving salbutamol ) .

armGroups.label: Salbutamol - Placebo
armGroups.description: salbutamol at M0 and M60 placebo administration

armGroups.label: Placebo - Salbutamol
armGroups.description: placebo at M0 and M60 salbutamol administration

interventions.name: Salbutamol
interventions.description: First administration of salbutamol or placebo at M0 and 2nd salbutamol or placebo at M60 (or M0 + 60 minutes)

interventions.name: Placebo
interventions.description: First administration of salbutamol or placebo at M0 and 2nd salbutamol or placebo at M60 (or M0 + 60 minutes)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Collection at the bedside spirometric values
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: gross change in forced expiratory volume in one second from the beginning of salbutamol or placebo and 15 min after the start

secondaryOutcomes.measure: gross change in vital capacity of peak expiratory flow rate, the ratio of FEV, forced expiratory flow
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: between the median baseline and 15 min after the start of treatment

secondaryOutcomes.measure: gross change in respiratory rate, heart rate and systolic and diastolic blood pressure
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: between baseline and 15 min after the start of treatment
between baseline and 15 min after the start of treatment

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Patient over 18 years old
* Patient with BPCO, defined:

  * by an irreversible obstructive syndrome
  * and\\or by different arguments (histories, symptoms, physical examination, thoracic radiography, gas of the blood)
* Decompensation of this BPCO in the form of acute respiratory failure
* No argument for a dominant acute left cardiac insufficiency
* Consent signed by the patient
* Patient with national health assurance

Exclusion Criteria:

* Contraindications in the not invasive ventilation
* Patient not volunteer for the realization of the spirometry
* Precautions for use of ß2 mimetic (engrave hyperthyroidism)
* Under guardianship patient or protection of justice
* Pregnant patient

locations.facility: Service de Réanimation Médicale - CHR d'Orléans

references.citation: Bodet-Contentin L, Guillon A, Boulain T, Frat JP, Garot D, Le Pennec D, Vecellio L, Ehrmann S, Giraudeau B, Tavernier E, Dequin PF. Salbutamol Nebulization During Noninvasive Ventilation in Exacerbated Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease Patients: A Randomized Controlled Trial. J Aerosol Med Pulm Drug Deliv. 2019 Jun;32(3):149-155. doi: 10.1089/jamp.2018.1484. Epub 2018 Dec 27.
